---
title: "model1binaryfinal?"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Model 1: Binary Admission Severity Classification

This model predicts COVID-19 severity at admission using clinical and metabolomic features. The binary target classes are `non_severe` and `severe`, where moderate patients are treated as non_severe, and severe/critical patients are combined as severe.

```{r}
library(reticulate)

py_require(c(
  "pandas",
  "numpy",
  "matplotlib",
  "seaborn",
  "scikit-learn",
  "xgboost",
  "joblib"
))
```

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SETUP
# ============================================================

import os
import re
import hashlib
import joblib
import warnings

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

from sklearn.model_selection import GroupKFold, GridSearchCV, cross_validate, cross_val_predict
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, LabelEncoder
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.feature_selection import SelectFromModel

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import ExtraTreesClassifier
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPClassifier
from sklearn.svm import SVC
from xgboost import XGBClassifier

from sklearn.metrics import (
    classification_report,
    ConfusionMatrixDisplay,
    roc_auc_score,
    average_precision_score,
    f1_score,
    balanced_accuracy_score,
    precision_score,
    recall_score
)

RANDOM_STATE = 42

CACHE_VERSION = "model1_severity_binary_same_model2_pipeline_v3"
CACHE_DIR = "cached_model1_severity_binary"
os.makedirs(CACHE_DIR, exist_ok=True)

sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
```

# Load Data

```{python}
# ============================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================

# Change this path if needed.
df = pd.read_csv("C:/Users/manna/OneDrive/Desktop/model1datamulti.csv")
pd.set_option("display.max_columns", None)

print("Raw data shape:", df.shape)
print("Columns:")
for col in df.columns:
    print(col)
```

# Create Severity Target

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CREATE MODEL 1 SEVERITY TARGET
# ============================================================

TARGET_COL_RAW = "Severity on admission (Moderate, severe, critical)"
GROUP_COL_RAW = "Sample ID"

# Clean common missing value markers.
df = df.replace(["-", "None", "", " ", "NA", "N/A", "nan", "NaN"], np.nan)

# Binary severity target:
# moderate = non_severe
# severe + critical = severe

df["severity_class"] = (
    df[TARGET_COL_RAW]
    .astype(str)
    .str.strip()
    .str.lower()
    .replace({
        "moderate": "non_severe",
        "severe": "severe",
        "critical": "severe"
    })
)

valid_classes = ["non_severe", "severe"]

# Keep only valid binary severity labels.
df_model = df[df["severity_class"].isin(valid_classes)].copy()

# Make a group ID. This dataset appears to have one row per patient/sample, so Sample ID is used.
# If Sample ID is missing, use row number to avoid breaking GroupKFold.
df_model["patient_group"] = df_model[GROUP_COL_RAW].astype(str)
df_model.loc[df_model[GROUP_COL_RAW].isna(), "patient_group"] = "row_" + df_model.index.astype(str)

print("Severity target counts:")
print(df_model["severity_class"].value_counts())

print("\nUnique patient/sample groups:")
print(df_model["patient_group"].nunique())
```

# Define Candidate Predictors

```{python}
# ============================================================
# DEFINE CANDIDATE PREDICTORS
# ============================================================

# Columns that should NOT be used as predictors because they are identifiers, labels,
# or outcome variables that may leak severity information.
exclude_cols = {
    GROUP_COL_RAW,
    TARGET_COL_RAW,
    "severity_class",
    "patient_group",
    "Survived",
    "Intubated",
    "Pneumonia diagnosis",
    "Kidney injury",
    "Liver injury",
    "Vascular injury",
    "CNS injury"
}

# Use all remaining admission clinical + metabolomic variables as candidate predictors.
# This keeps the modelling approach aligned with Model 2 while adapting preprocessing to this dataset.
feature_cols_raw = [c for c in df_model.columns if c not in exclude_cols]

print("Initial candidate predictors:", len(feature_cols_raw))
print("Excluded columns:", sorted([c for c in exclude_cols if c in df_model.columns]))
```

# Clean Model Dataset

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CLEAN MODEL DATA
# ============================================================

model_df = df_model[["patient_group", "severity_class"] + feature_cols_raw].copy()

# Helper function to convert messy numeric columns such as '< LOD' and '0.33 (ug/mL)'.
def extract_numeric_series(series):
    cleaned = series.astype(str).str.strip()
    cleaned = cleaned.replace({
        "< LOD": np.nan,
        "<LOD": np.nan,
        "LOD": np.nan,
        "nan": np.nan,
        "None": np.nan,
        "-": np.nan,
        "": np.nan
    })
    extracted = cleaned.str.extract(r"([-+]?\d*\.?\d+(?:[eE][-+]?\d+)?)", expand=False)
    return pd.to_numeric(extracted, errors="coerce")

# Convert numeric-looking object columns to numeric.
# Truly categorical columns, such as Sex, are one-hot encoded.
feature_cols_current = [c for c in model_df.columns if c not in ["patient_group", "severity_class"]]
categorical_cols = []

for col in feature_cols_current:
    if model_df[col].dtype == "object":
        numeric_version = extract_numeric_series(model_df[col])
        non_missing_original = model_df[col].notna().sum()
        numeric_success = numeric_version.notna().sum()
        success_ratio = numeric_success / max(non_missing_original, 1)

        if success_ratio >= 0.50:
            model_df[col] = numeric_version
        else:
            categorical_cols.append(col)
    else:
        model_df[col] = pd.to_numeric(model_df[col], errors="coerce")

print("Categorical columns one-hot encoded:", categorical_cols)

if len(categorical_cols) > 0:
    model_df = pd.get_dummies(
        model_df,
        columns=categorical_cols,
        drop_first=True,
        dummy_na=False
    )

feature_cols = [c for c in model_df.columns if c not in ["patient_group", "severity_class"]]

# Remove features with too much missingness. Proposal used >30%; adjust if needed.
MISSING_THRESHOLD = 0.30
missing_rate = model_df[feature_cols].isna().mean()
drop_missing = missing_rate[missing_rate > MISSING_THRESHOLD].index.tolist()
model_df = model_df.drop(columns=drop_missing)

feature_cols = [c for c in model_df.columns if c not in ["patient_group", "severity_class"]]

# Remove constant columns.
constant_cols = [
    c for c in feature_cols
    if model_df[c].nunique(dropna=True) <= 1
]
model_df = model_df.drop(columns=constant_cols)

feature_cols = [c for c in model_df.columns if c not in ["patient_group", "severity_class"]]

print("Final model dataframe shape:", model_df.shape)
print("Dropped high-missing features:", len(drop_missing))
print("Dropped constant features:", len(constant_cols))

print("\nFinal class counts:")
print(model_df["severity_class"].value_counts())

print("\nFinal unique groups:")
print(model_df["patient_group"].nunique())

print("\nFinal predictors:", len(feature_cols))
```

# Prepare Modelling Objects

```{python}
# ============================================================
# PREPARE X, y, GROUPS
# ============================================================

X = model_df.drop(columns=["patient_group", "severity_class"])
X.columns = X.columns.astype(str)

# Fixed binary class order for interpretability.
target_names = ["non_severe", "severe"]

label_encoder = LabelEncoder()
label_encoder.fit(target_names)

y_raw = model_df["severity_class"].astype(str)
y = label_encoder.transform(y_raw)
groups = model_df["patient_group"].astype(str)

print("Class mapping:")
for cls, encoded in zip(label_encoder.classes_, label_encoder.transform(label_encoder.classes_)):
    print(f"{cls} -> {encoded}")

print("\nSamples:", X.shape[0])
print("Predictors:", X.shape[1])
print("Unique patient/sample groups:", groups.nunique())

print("\nClass proportions:")
print(y_raw.value_counts(normalize=True).reindex(target_names))

n_classes = len(label_encoder.classes_)
severe_class = label_encoder.transform(["severe"])[0]
```

# Class Distribution Plot

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CLASS DISTRIBUTION PLOT
# ============================================================

class_counts = y_raw.value_counts().reindex(target_names)

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
sns.barplot(x=class_counts.index, y=class_counts.values)

plt.title("Model 1 Binary Admission Severity Class Distribution")
plt.xlabel("Severity Class")
plt.ylabel("Number of Samples")

for i, value in enumerate(class_counts.values):
    plt.text(i, value + 0.5, str(value), ha="center")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Define Cache Key

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CACHE KEY
# ============================================================

cache_string = (
    CACHE_VERSION
    + str(X.shape)
    + str(list(X.columns))
    + str(pd.Series(y).value_counts().to_dict())
)

cache_key = hashlib.md5(cache_string.encode()).hexdigest()[:10]

print("Cache key:", cache_key)
```

# Feature Selection Object

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FEATURE SELECTION OBJECT
# ============================================================

# Same feature selection logic as Model 2:
# Extra Trees ranks predictors, SelectFromModel keeps the top selected features.
# This happens inside the CV pipeline to avoid feature-selection leakage.

max_selected_features = min(100, X.shape[1])

et_selector = SelectFromModel(
    ExtraTreesClassifier(
        n_estimators=250,
        class_weight="balanced",
        random_state=RANDOM_STATE,
        max_features="sqrt",
        min_samples_leaf=2,
        n_jobs=1
    ),
    max_features=max_selected_features
)
```

# Define Six Models

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SIX MODEL DEFINITIONS + HYPERPARAMETER GRIDS
# ============================================================

# Same model families and tuning logic as Model 2, adapted for binary severity prediction.

model_specs = {

    "Extra Trees": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", ExtraTreesClassifier(
                class_weight="balanced",
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE,
                n_jobs=1
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features":[5, 10, 20, 30, 50, 75, 100],
            "model__n_estimators": [250, 400],
            "model__max_features": ["sqrt", 0.3],
            "model__min_samples_leaf": [2, 3],
            "model__max_depth": [None, 8]
        }
    },

    "XGBoost": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", XGBClassifier(
                objective="binary:logistic",
                eval_metric="logloss",
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE,
                n_jobs=1
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features": [5, 10, 20, 30, 50, 75, 100],
            "model__n_estimators": [150, 250],
            "model__max_depth": [2, 3],
            "model__learning_rate": [0.03, 0.05],
            "model__subsample": [0.8, 1.0],
            "model__colsample_bytree": [0.8, 1.0]
        }
    },

    "Elastic Net Logistic Regression": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", LogisticRegression(
                max_iter=5000,
                class_weight="balanced",
                penalty="elasticnet",
                solver="saga",
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features":[5, 10, 20, 30, 50, 75, 100],
            "model__C": [0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0],
            "model__l1_ratio": [0.25, 0.5, 0.75]
        }
    },

    "SVM RBF": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", SVC(
                kernel="rbf",
                probability=True,
                class_weight="balanced",
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features": [5, 10, 20, 30, 50, 75, 100],
            "model__C": [0.5, 1.0, 2.0],
            "model__gamma": ["scale", 0.01]
        }
    },

    "Gaussian Naive Bayes": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", GaussianNB())
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features": [5, 10, 20, 30, 50, 75, 100],
            "model__var_smoothing": [1e-9, 1e-8, 1e-7, 1e-6]
        }
    },

    "MLP Neural Network": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", MLPClassifier(
                activation="relu",
                solver="adam",
                early_stopping=True,
                validation_fraction=0.2,
                n_iter_no_change=30,
                max_iter=1500,
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features":[5, 10, 20, 30, 50, 75, 100],
            "model__hidden_layer_sizes": [
                (16,),
                (32,),
                (32, 16),
                (64, 32),
                (64, 32, 16)
            ],
            "model__alpha": [0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1],
            "model__learning_rate_init": [0.001, 0.0005, 0.0001],
            "model__batch_size": [16, 32, 64]
        }
    }
}

print("Models included:")
for model_name in model_specs.keys():
    print("-", model_name)
```

# Cached Hyperparameter Tuning

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CACHED GROUPED HYPERPARAMETER TUNING
# ============================================================

# Same optimisation metric as Model 2: Macro F1.
# This is appropriate for binary severity classification because both classes matter.

inner_cv = GroupKFold(n_splits=5)

tuned_results = {}
best_estimators = {}

for name, spec in model_specs.items():

    safe_name = name.replace(" ", "_").replace("/", "_")
    cache_path = os.path.join(CACHE_DIR, f"{cache_key}_{safe_name}_best_model.joblib")

    if os.path.exists(cache_path):
        print(f"\nLoading cached tuned model: {name}")

        cached = joblib.load(cache_path)

        best_estimators[name] = cached["best_estimator"]
        tuned_results[name] = cached["tuned_result"]

        continue

    print(f"\nTuning model: {name}")

    search = GridSearchCV(
        estimator=spec["pipeline"],
        param_grid=spec["param_grid"],
        scoring="f1_macro",
        cv=inner_cv,
        n_jobs=1,
        error_score="raise",
        refit=True
    )

    search.fit(X, y, groups=groups)

    tuned_results[name] = {
        "Best CV Macro F1": search.best_score_,
        "Best Params": search.best_params_
    }

    best_estimators[name] = search.best_estimator_

    joblib.dump({
        "best_estimator": search.best_estimator_,
        "tuned_result": tuned_results[name]
    }, cache_path)

    print("Best CV Macro F1:", round(search.best_score_, 3))
    print("Saved cache:", cache_path)

tuned_results_df = pd.DataFrame(tuned_results).T.sort_values(
    "Best CV Macro F1",
    ascending=False
)

print("\nHyperparameter tuning summary:")
print(tuned_results_df)
```

# Final Grouped Cross-Validated Model Comparison

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FINAL GROUPED CV MODEL COMPARISON
# ============================================================

outer_cv = GroupKFold(n_splits=5)

final_results_cache = os.path.join(CACHE_DIR, f"{cache_key}_final_results.joblib")

if os.path.exists(final_results_cache):

    print("Loading cached final CV results.")
    final_results_df = joblib.load(final_results_cache)

else:

    final_results = {}

    for name, estimator in best_estimators.items():

        print("\nEvaluating tuned model:", name)

        scores = cross_validate(
            estimator,
            X,
            y,
            groups=groups,
            cv=outer_cv,
            scoring={
                "balanced_accuracy": "balanced_accuracy",
                "macro_f1": "f1_macro",
                "auroc": "roc_auc",
                "precision_macro": "precision_macro",
                "recall_macro": "recall_macro"
            },
            n_jobs=1,
            error_score="raise"
        )

        final_results[name] = {
            "Balanced Accuracy Mean": scores["test_balanced_accuracy"].mean(),
            "Balanced Accuracy SD": scores["test_balanced_accuracy"].std(),
            "Macro F1 Mean": scores["test_macro_f1"].mean(),
            "Macro F1 SD": scores["test_macro_f1"].std(),
            "AUROC Mean": scores["test_auroc"].mean(),
            "AUROC SD": scores["test_auroc"].std(),
            "Precision Macro Mean": scores["test_precision_macro"].mean(),
            "Precision Macro SD": scores["test_precision_macro"].std(),
            "Recall Macro Mean": scores["test_recall_macro"].mean(),
            "Recall Macro SD": scores["test_recall_macro"].std()
        }

    final_results_df = pd.DataFrame(final_results).T.sort_values(
        "Macro F1 Mean",
        ascending=False
    )

    joblib.dump(final_results_df, final_results_cache)

print("\nFinal grouped-CV model comparison:")
print(final_results_df)

print("\nRounded report table:")
print(final_results_df.round(3))
```

# Model Comparison Plots

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FOUR SEPARATE MODEL PERFORMANCE BOXPLOTS
# ============================================================

fold_rows = []

for name, estimator in best_estimators.items():

    scores = cross_validate(
        estimator,
        X,
        y,
        groups=groups,
        cv=outer_cv,
        scoring={
            "Macro F1": "f1_macro",
            "AUROC": "roc_auc",
            "Balanced Accuracy": "balanced_accuracy",
            "Macro Recall": "recall_macro"
        },
        n_jobs=1,
        error_score="raise"
    )

    for i in range(5):
        fold_rows.append({
            "Model": name,
            "Macro F1": scores["test_Macro F1"][i],
            "AUROC": scores["test_AUROC"][i],
            "Balanced Accuracy": scores["test_Balanced Accuracy"][i],
            "Macro Recall": scores["test_Macro Recall"][i]
        })

fold_df = pd.DataFrame(fold_rows)

rename_map = {
    "Elastic Net Logistic Regression": "Elastic Net",
    "MLP Neural Network": "MLP",
    "Extra Trees": "Extra Trees",
    "XGBoost": "XGBoost",
    "SVM RBF": "SVM",
    "Gaussian Naive Bayes": "Gaussian NB"
}

fold_df["Model"] = fold_df["Model"].map(rename_map)

model_order = [
    "Elastic Net",
    "MLP",
    "Extra Trees",
    "XGBoost",
    "SVM",
    "Gaussian NB"
]

palette = sns.color_palette("Set2", n_colors=len(model_order))
palette_dict = dict(zip(model_order, palette))

metrics = [
    "Macro F1",
    "AUROC",
    "Balanced Accuracy",
    "Macro Recall"
]

for metric in metrics:

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6.5))

    sns.boxplot(
        data=fold_df,
        x="Model",
        y=metric,
        order=model_order,
        palette=palette_dict,
        width=0.6,
        linewidth=1.2
    )

    sns.stripplot(
        data=fold_df,
        x="Model",
        y=metric,
        order=model_order,
        color="black",
        size=5.5,
        alpha=0.55,
        jitter=0.12
    )

    plt.title(f"Grouped Cross-Validation {metric}", fontsize=17, fontweight="bold")
    plt.xlabel("")
    plt.ylabel("Score", fontsize=13)
    plt.ylim(0.0, 1.05)

    plt.xticks(rotation=20, ha="right", fontsize=11)
    plt.yticks(fontsize=11)

    plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.35)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

# Select Best Model

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SELECT BEST MODEL
# ============================================================

best_model_name = final_results_df.index[0]
best_pipe = best_estimators[best_model_name]

print("Best final model:", best_model_name)

print("\nBest tuned parameters:")
print(tuned_results_df.loc[best_model_name, "Best Params"])
```

# Cross-Validated Predictions for Best Model

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CACHED CROSS-VALIDATED PREDICTIONS FOR BEST MODEL
# ============================================================

safe_best_name = best_model_name.replace(" ", "_").replace("/", "_")
pred_cache_path = os.path.join(CACHE_DIR, f"{cache_key}_{safe_best_name}_predictions.joblib")

if os.path.exists(pred_cache_path):

    print("Loading cached cross-validated predictions.")

    pred_cache = joblib.load(pred_cache_path)

    y_pred_cv = pred_cache["y_pred_cv"]
    y_proba_cv = pred_cache["y_proba_cv"]

else:

    print("Generating cross-validated predictions for best model.")

    y_pred_cv = cross_val_predict(
        best_pipe,
        X,
        y,
        groups=groups,
        cv=outer_cv,
        n_jobs=1,
        method="predict"
    )

    y_proba_cv = cross_val_predict(
        best_pipe,
        X,
        y,
        groups=groups,
        cv=outer_cv,
        n_jobs=1,
        method="predict_proba"
    )

    joblib.dump({
        "y_pred_cv": y_pred_cv,
        "y_proba_cv": y_proba_cv
    }, pred_cache_path)
```

```{python}
# ============================================================
# MODEL 1 THRESHOLD TUNING
# ============================================================

# severe = 1
positive_class = label_encoder.transform(["severe"])[0]
positive_index = list(best_pipe.classes_).index(positive_class)

severity_probs = y_proba_cv[:, positive_index]

threshold_grid = np.arange(0.05, 0.96, 0.01)

threshold_rows = []

for threshold in threshold_grid:

    pred_labels = (severity_probs >= threshold).astype(int)

    threshold_rows.append({
        "threshold": threshold,
        "macro_f1": f1_score(y, pred_labels, average="macro"),
        "balanced_accuracy": balanced_accuracy_score(y, pred_labels),
        "severe_precision": precision_score(y, pred_labels, zero_division=0),
        "severe_recall": recall_score(y, pred_labels, zero_division=0),
        "non_severe_recall": recall_score(1 - y, 1 - pred_labels, zero_division=0)
    })

threshold_df = pd.DataFrame(threshold_rows)

best_threshold_row = threshold_df.sort_values("macro_f1", ascending=False).iloc[0]
best_threshold = best_threshold_row["threshold"]

print("Best threshold based on Macro F1:")
print(best_threshold_row)

threshold_pred_labels = (severity_probs >= best_threshold).astype(int)

print("\nClassification report using tuned threshold:")
print(classification_report(
    y,
    threshold_pred_labels,
    target_names=label_encoder.classes_
))

ConfusionMatrixDisplay.from_predictions(
    y,
    threshold_pred_labels,
    display_labels=label_encoder.classes_,
    cmap="Blues"
)

plt.title(f"Tuned Threshold Confusion Matrix - {best_model_name} - Threshold {best_threshold:.2f}")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

threshold_df.to_csv("model1_threshold_sensitivity.csv", index=False)
print("Saved model1_threshold_sensitivity.csv")
```

# Best Model Classification Report

```{python}
# ============================================================
# BEST MODEL CLASSIFICATION REPORT
# ============================================================

print("Classification report for best model:")
print(classification_report(
    y,
    y_pred_cv,
    target_names=label_encoder.classes_
))

best_macro_f1 = f1_score(y, y_pred_cv, average="macro")
best_balanced_accuracy = balanced_accuracy_score(y, y_pred_cv)
best_precision_macro = precision_score(y, y_pred_cv, average="macro", zero_division=0)
best_recall_macro = recall_score(y, y_pred_cv, average="macro", zero_division=0)

best_auroc = roc_auc_score(y, y_proba_cv[:, 1])
best_avg_precision = average_precision_score(y, y_proba_cv[:, 1])

print("Grouped-CV Macro F1:", round(best_macro_f1, 3))
print("Grouped-CV Balanced Accuracy:", round(best_balanced_accuracy, 3))
print("Grouped-CV Macro Precision:", round(best_precision_macro, 3))
print("Grouped-CV Macro Recall:", round(best_recall_macro, 3))
print("Grouped-CV AUROC:", round(best_auroc, 3))
print("Grouped-CV Average Precision:", round(best_avg_precision, 3))
```

# Confusion Matrix

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CONFUSION MATRIX
# ============================================================

ConfusionMatrixDisplay.from_predictions(
    y,
    y_pred_cv,
    display_labels=label_encoder.classes_,
    cmap="Blues"
)

plt.title(f"Grouped-CV Confusion Matrix - {best_model_name}")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Probability Confidence Plot

```{python}
# ============================================================
# PREDICTED CONFIDENCE DISTRIBUTION
# ============================================================

confidence_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Predicted confidence": np.max(y_proba_cv, axis=1),
    "True class": label_encoder.inverse_transform(y),
    "Predicted class": label_encoder.inverse_transform(y_pred_cv)
})
confidence_df["Correct"] = confidence_df["True class"] == confidence_df["Predicted class"]

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
sns.kdeplot(
    data=confidence_df,
    x="Predicted confidence",
    hue="Correct",
    fill=True,
    common_norm=False,
    alpha=0.4
)

plt.title("Predicted Confidence Distribution by Correctness")
plt.xlabel("Maximum predicted class probability")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Feature Importance

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FEATURE IMPORTANCE
# ============================================================

best_pipe.fit(X, y)

selector = best_pipe.named_steps["feature_selection"]
model = best_pipe.named_steps["model"]

selected_features = X.columns[selector.get_support()]

if hasattr(model, "feature_importances_"):
    importance_values = model.feature_importances_

elif hasattr(model, "coef_"):
    importance_values = np.mean(np.abs(model.coef_), axis=0)

else:
    importance_values = np.repeat(np.nan, len(selected_features))

importance_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": selected_features,
    "importance": importance_values
}).sort_values("importance", ascending=False)

print("Top selected predictors:")
print(importance_df.head(30))

top_n = 15

plot_importance = importance_df.head(top_n).copy()

plot_importance["short_feature"] = [
    f[:38] + "..." if len(f) > 38 else f
    for f in plot_importance["feature"]
]

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 7))

plt.barh(
    plot_importance["short_feature"][::-1],
    plot_importance["importance"][::-1]
)

plt.xlabel("Importance")
plt.ylabel("")
plt.title(f"Top {top_n} Selected Predictors - {best_model_name}")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Save Report Tables

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SAVE REPORT TABLES
# ============================================================

final_results_df.to_csv("model1_binary_severity_grouped_cv_results.csv")
tuned_results_df.to_csv("model1_binary_severity_tuned_hyperparameters.csv")
importance_df.to_csv("model1_binary_severity_feature_importance.csv", index=False)

print("Saved report tables:")
print("- model1_binary_severity_grouped_cv_results.csv")
print("- model1_binary_severity_tuned_hyperparameters.csv")
print("- model1_binary_severity_feature_importance.csv")
```

# Final Summary

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================

print("================ FINAL SUMMARY ================")

print("Aim: Predict binary admission severity class from clinical and metabolomic predictors")
print("Target: non_severe = moderate; severe = severe + critical")
print("Leakage control: GroupKFold by Sample ID / patient group")

print("Samples:", X.shape[0])
print("Unique groups:", groups.nunique())
print("Predictors before selection:", X.shape[1])

print("Best model:", best_model_name)

print("Grouped-CV Macro F1:", round(best_macro_f1, 3))
print("Grouped-CV Balanced Accuracy:", round(best_balanced_accuracy, 3))
print("Grouped-CV AUROC:", round(best_auroc, 3))
print("Grouped-CV Average Precision:", round(best_avg_precision, 3))

print("\nBest model grouped CV performance:")
print(final_results_df.loc[[best_model_name]].to_string())

print("================================================")
```

# Interpretation Notes

This Model 1 workflow uses the same machine-learning structure as Model 2, including grouped cross-validation, embedded tree-based feature selection, six candidate model families, and Macro F1-score for hyperparameter optimisation. The main difference is that the target is now binary admission severity classification rather than multiclass severity prediction.

The binary target combines severe and critical patients into a single severe class, while moderate patients are treated as non_severe. Model performance is interpreted using Macro F1-score, balanced accuracy, AUROC, macro precision, macro recall, and the confusion matrix.

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FULL FINAL RESULTS TABLE
# ============================================================

pd.set_option("display.max_columns", None)
pd.set_option("display.width", 200)

print(final_results_df.round(3))
```

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SAVE MODEL 1 DEPLOYMENT FEATURE PANEL
# ============================================================

deployment_features = selected_features.tolist()

deployment_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": deployment_features
})

deployment_df.to_csv(
    "model1_deployment_features.csv",
    index=False
)

print("\nSaved deployment feature panel:")
print("model1_deployment_features.csv")

print("\nNumber of deployment features:")
print(len(deployment_features))
```
